query: Narendra Modi Union cabinet ministers heatwave instructions July 2026 government action plan measures

mako.raw:
{"query":"Narendra Modi Union cabinet ministers heatwave instructions July 2026 government action plan measures","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1624,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://sg.news.yahoo.com/indian-pm-modi-urges-states-145928102.html","title":"Indian PM Modi urges states to prepare heat action plans as country swelters - Yahoo News Singapore","content":"According to Indian media reports, he advised states and union territories to prepare heat action plans at state, city and district levels."},{"url":"https://www.business-standard.com/india-news/pm-modi-stresses-whole-of-nation-approach-to-tackle-heatwave-vaishnaw-126052701154_1.html","title":"PM Modi has stressed whole-of-nation approach to tackle heatwave: Vaishnaw | India News - Business Standard","content":"Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday urged all Union cabinet ministers to ensure that their respective ministries and departments take necessary steps to mitigate the impact of the prevailing heatwave conditions across the country.
Addressing a media briefing after the cabinet meeting, Union Minister Ashvini Vaishnaw said the prime minister stressed the need for a "whole-of-nation" approach to deal with the extreme heat situation.
Vaishnaw said the prime minister directed all ministries to take appropriate action to protect people from the heatwave, particularly departments such as health and water resources.
He was clear that such major challenges should be addressed through a whole-of-nation approach, with the Centre, states and citizens working together," the minister said.
Asked about the measures the government plans to take, Vaishnaw said individual ministries and departments would determine their respective courses of action and added that the Centre has also reached out to state governments."},{"url":"https://www.aninews.in/news/national/general-news/union-cabinet-discusses-prevailing-heatwave-situation-pm-modi-calls-for-whole-of-the-nation-approach20260527163836","title":"Union Cabinet discusses prevailing heatwave situation, PM Modi calls for whole-of-the-nation approach","content":"The meeting of the Union Cabinet discussed steps that can be implemented within the Ministry of Health, the Water Resources sector, and other related departments.
"The Prime Minister stated very clearly that when addressing major challenges of this nature, we must approach them with a 'whole-of-the-nation' spirit, which includes the Centre, State government, and the citizens," he said.
Earlier, PM Modi urged citizens to take precautions against the rising temperatures across the country, advising people to stay hydrated, carry water while stepping outdoors and extend help to those in need as several states continue to reel under intense summer heat.
The weather office said Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh and Delhi are expected to continue experiencing heatwave conditions on May 27 and 28.
Severe heatwave conditions are also likely in isolated areas of Punjab, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh."},{"url":"https://m.economictimes.com/news/india/union-home-minister-amit-shah-said-the-government-is-working-towards-reducing-heat-wave-related-deaths-and-bring-it-to-zero/articleshow/131102323.cms","title":"Govt aims to reduce heat wave-linked deaths: Amit Shah - The Economic Times","content":"Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced a significant government goal.
He said under the leadership of PM Narendra Modi, India has not only worked towards disaster risk reduction but also moving towards zero casualty."},{"url":"https://www.deccanchronicle.com/nation/pm-modi-calls-for-whole-of-the-nation-approach-to-tackle-heatwave-1959478","title":"PM Modi Calls for ‘Whole-of-the-Nation’ Approach to Tackle Heatwave","content":"New Delhi: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday called for a “whole-of-the-nation” approach to tackle the prevailing heatwave as the Union Cabinet discussed measures to provide relief to citizens.
Briefing the media after the meeting, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said the heatwave situation was reviewed in detail, with discussions focusing on steps that various ministries and departments can take within their domains, including health and water resources."}]}
